steps.title: Spot the relationship
steps.content: Breton's realist style emphasizes accuracy and ordinary people. Gleyre's romantic style idealized subjects with staged settings and unblemished skin. The blank describes how the realist style relates to romantic conventions — and the contrast between the two styles signals opposition, not continuation.

steps.title: Eliminate and confirm
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rejected': means turned away or refused — the realist style abandoned romantic conventions entirely, which matches the sharp contrast the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'extended': means built on or expanded — but the realist and romantic styles are opposites; realism did not continue romantic conventions. ✗
  • Choice C — 'imitated': means copied — but the passage contrasts the two styles, so the realist style cannot have imitated the romantic style. ✗
  • Choice D — 'epitomized': means was the perfect example of — but that would make realism a prime instance of romanticism, which contradicts the passage. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
